methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $270M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
REGENT Craft is a Series A transportation company founded by Billy Thalheimer and Mike Klinker, currently valued at $270M post-money with $60M raised to date. The broader transportation sector includes eight private peers, among them Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and Pipedream, though no public comparables exist. The narrow peer set within same-stage and adjacent-stage bands proved insufficient to support peer-median triangulation, which would have required three or more closely-matched datapoints.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here and serves as the primary valuation anchor. Using the $270M last post-money as a base, a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement implies a valuation of $540M. This methodology reflects empirical progression patterns observed across similar-stage rounds in frontier transportation, though it does not account for company-specific execution risk or sector tailwinds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $459M to $621M, centered at $540M. The range incorporates ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression median. Key uncertainties include the strength of REGENT Craft's competitive position relative to better-capitalized peers like Rivian, unit economics validation, and regulatory pathway clarity for the underlying technology. The single-method foundation of this range reflects data constraints; peer-comparable support would materially strengthen conviction in any valuation conclusion.
